events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence check for bearings & seals industrial site. Observed field condition: over-greasing (purged seal). Work actively in-progress. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Over-greasing purged seal is non-compliant/defective, can damage seals, cause overheating, contamination. Decision fail. Risk high? Over-greasing can cause seal failure, contamination, bearing damage, maybe critical? In industrial heavy machinery, over-greasing leading to purged seal is high risk. component_conditions installed-defective, maybe non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 70? value not asked 0. sound_source etc default empty strings/0. order: sequence violation: lubrication should follow specified quantity/regression method; seal integrity check before greasing; verify grease type/quantity; clean; inspect seal after. Need list step-ids. workable maybe empty or activities that can still start? The task asks state what must happen before work can continue. actions: stop work, clean excess grease, inspect/replace damaged seal, verify correct grease quantity per manufacturer, re-lubricate per SKF/Timken guidelines, inspect bearing housing, document, get inspection sign-off. references: SKF/Timken mounting/lubrication guides, maybe OSHA? But references field code or standard anchors. Use "SKF bearing maintenance handbook", "Timken lubrication guidelines". Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

    "Excess grease indicates lubrication quantity/sequence not followed",
    "Potential seal damage and contamination risk to bearing"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop greasing immediately",
    "Clean all purged/excess grease from seal and housing",
    "Inspect seal for damage or displacement; replace if compromised",
    "Verify correct grease type and regrease quantity per SKF/Timken guidance",
    "Relubricate using controlled method and correct volume",
    "Re-inspect seal and housing after regreasing",
    "Document corrective action and obtain inspection sign-off before continuing"
  ],
  "rationale": "Over-greasing that purges the seal violates the correct bearing lubrication sequence and can damage the seal, force contaminants into the bearing, and reduce service life. Work must be halted until the seal is inspected, excess grease is removed, and relubrication is performed to the specified quantity.",

    "SKF bearing maintenance and lubrication guidelines",
    "Timken mounting and lubrication guide"
  ]
